content.text:
<user_query>
i just setup cron-job.org to still awake the render server but the jitter is about 25 seconds, is this normal?
</user_query>

Checking your Render setup and how the wake ping is configured so I can explain whether ~25s jitter is expected.

[REDACTED]

input.search_term: cron-job.org scheduling jitter accuracy delay seconds
input.explanation: Check whether cron-job.org is known for ~25s scheduling jitter

input.search_term: Render free tier cold start wake up time seconds 2024 2025
input.explanation: Check typical Render free tier spin-up latency when pinged from cron

content.text:
Yes — **25 seconds can be normal**, but it depends on which number you’re looking at in cron-job.org.

## Two different numbers

cron-job.org’s history shows both:

| Field | What it means |
|--------|----------------|
| **Jitter** | How early/late the ping *started* vs the scheduled time (in ms). ~25s ≈ 25,000 ms. |
| **Duration** | How long the HTTP request took end-to-end — often much longer if Render was asleep. |

If **duration** is ~25–60s but **jitter** is small, that’s almost certainly **Render cold start**, not cron-job.org being late.

Your health check is `/api/auth/status` (from `render.yaml`). For an unauthenticated ping it’s lightweight — no DB — so slow responses usually mean the container was spinning up, not your app doing heavy work.

## What’s “normal” for each

**cron-job.org jitter (~25s):**  
Their [FAQ](https://cron-job.org/en/faq/) says they don’t guarantee exact timing; small delays are expected, especially at peak. **~25s is on the high side but not crazy** for a free external cron. Typical is often a few seconds; tens of seconds can happen.

**Render free tier wake time:**  
Render [documents ~1 minute](https://render.com/docs/free) to spin back up after 15 minutes idle. **25s fits that range** (often 30–60s depending on Docker image, region, load).

## For “keep awake” specifically

Render spins down after **15 minutes with no traffic**. So:

- Ping every **14 minutes** → you’re right on the edge; **25s late jitter can let it sleep**.
- Safer: ping every **10–12 minutes** so jitter + occasional misses don’t cross the 15‑minute gap.

Example: `*/10 * * * *` (every 10 minutes) with URL  
`https://<your-service>.onrender.com/api/auth/status`

## Quick sanity check

In cron-job.org execution history for one run:

- **Jitter ~25s, duration < 5s** → scheduling slop; annoying but fine for keep-awake if your interval has margin.
- **Jitter small, duration ~25–60s** → Render was cold; the cron worked, but something let the service idle past 15 minutes (interval too long, failed ping, or spin-down anyway).

If you share your cron interval and whether 25s is jitter vs duration, we can say whether you need a tighter schedule or a different pinger — but **25s alone isn’t a sign anything is broken**.

[REDACTED]
